title: Newspapers & serials: title, issue and article
intro: A serial nests cleanly in RiC: the title is an aggregation of issues, and each issue is made of articles. This scenario branches on a single issue vs the whole run, models articles as Record Parts, and attaches the publisher.
question: Is a newspaper title a Record Set, each issue a Record, and each article a Record Part?

steps.prompt: A single issue, or the whole newspaper title?

choices.label: A single issue
choices.why: Good — one issue is one Record; its articles are Record Parts of it.

choices.label: The whole title
choices.why: Good — a title (all its issues) is a Record Set, each issue a member Record.

choices.label: A Record Part
choices.why: Not the title. An article inside an issue is a Record Part; the title is the aggregation (Record Set).

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. One dated issue is a Record; its articles are Record Parts; the printed/scanned copy is an Instantiation.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one issue — the Set is the whole title.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: That's the physical/scanned copy. The issue's content is a Record.

capture.comment: Create the issue as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. The title aggregates all its issues — a Record Set, each issue a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a title is decades of distinct issues. Use a Record Set.

choices.label: Activity
choices.why: Doesn't fit. Publishing is an activity, but the title itself is the aggregation of issues.

capture.comment: Create the title as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: A notable article needs its own description (byline, subject). How do you model it?

choices.label: Record Part
choices.why: Fits. An article is a component with its own content inside an issue — a Record Part, carrying its own author and subject. Describe the articles you need; the rest stay implicit in the issue.

choices.label: A separate Record
choices.why: Usually too much — an article only makes sense inside its issue. Use a Record Part (unless it's syndicated and catalogued independently).

choices.label: An Instantiation
choices.why: Doesn't fit — an article is content, not a carrier.

capture.comment: Create the article as a Record Part of the issue.

choices.label: Corporate body (Agent) + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The publishing house is a Corporate Body — an Agent. Relate the title/issue to it with has_creator; the same Agent links every title it published.

choices.label: Put the masthead text in the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The publisher should be a reconcilable Agent so its whole output is findable.

capture.comment: Create the publisher as an Agent (corporate body).
Link the title/issue to the publisher with has_creator.

outcome.verdict: Title, issues, articles and publisher — one serial graph.
outcome.summary: The title is a Record Set; each issue a Record; notable articles are Record Parts; the publisher a reusable Agent. The serial is navigable top-to-bottom and findable by article author and subject — not just by reel or bound volume.

links.label: Record Set (RiC-E03) in the model navigator
See real RiC data in the graph viewer
